what_it_does: An acceptance test script for runtime/cognition.py that exercises resolve_address scheme dispatching and run_items fan semantics, verifying run:// template materialization, cas:// and bare-name resolution, fail-loud unknown schemes, one-role-over-N-units execution, per-unit output addresses, live-or-mocked transport, barrier failure, declared skip, and additive preservation of existing functions.
observations: Uses a global PASS counter and a simple check(label, cond) helper instead of a formal test framework.
Seeds run:// references via _seed using FsStore put_content and set_ref.
Tests both a live resident 4B transport and a mocked cognition.client.complete fallback to avoid false greens.
Contains extensive inline comments that read as a design specification for resolve_address and run_items.
Verifies skill:// is now a registered resolvable scheme and that unregistered schemes like foo:// raise extensible-seam errors.
Checks that a missing run:// address raises by default but returns None when on_missing='skip' is declared.
Asserts run_items emits exactly two events per fan: one cognition.items rollup and one op.run run-index emit.
Verifies per-unit outputs land at run://<turn>/<role>/<i> and are read back into ItemsResult.resolved.
Monkey-patches fabric client.complete inside try/finally blocks for mock branches.
Checks run_role signature and presence of run_swarm/run_jury/run_reduce to enforce additive change.
standouts: The file doubles as an executable specification with dense design rationale in comments.
It uses a live-or-mocked transport strategy so acceptance can run without the resident model.
It enforces additive change by asserting existing cognition functions remain unchanged.
conventions: Imperative module-level test script with global state.
Inline helper functions and small mock classes defined near their use sites.
Heavy use of comments to document intent and contracts.
Noqa E402 annotations for sys.path imports.
Monkey-patching for dependency substitution in mock branches.
concerns: Global mutable state (PASS, store, reg) shared across test phases.
Repeated nearly identical mock classes (_Validated, _V, _V2) and mock functions (_mock, _m, _m2).
Acceptance depends on an external resident 4B service for the live path.
No formal test runner or fixture isolation; failures stop the script via assert.
Module-level namespace pollution from test variables and mock definitions.
notes: A self-contained acceptance script that specifies and verifies the new resolve_address and run_items primitives while guarding against regressions in existing cognition functions.
questions: What is the exact signature and return type of resolve_address beyond the arguments used here?
How is run_items implemented with respect to concurrency, the VramGate, swarm_slots pool, and barrier?
What does the ItemsResult dataclass contain beyond .runs, .resolved, .skipped, .role_id, and .addresses?
What is the full schema and behavior of the focus role used in the fan test?
How does the skill registry integrate with resolve_address for skill:// addresses?
What are the precise contracts of the fabric transport and client.complete functions being mocked?
What do cognition.RESIDENT_BASE_URL and RESIDENT_MODEL refer to?
What is the #54 storage-discovery emit contract beyond the two events checked here?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file content: it proves resolve_address and run_items behavior, including scheme dispatch, axis inversion, fail-loud semantics, and additive preservation.
apparent_themes: Fail-loud design with explicit declared-skip escape hatch.
Extensible addressing seam for future schemes.
Axis inversion: one role over many inputs versus many roles over one context.
Acceptance testing that can degrade to mocked dependencies.
Driver-only telemetry with no governance verbs.
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock down the contract of two new cognition primitives, serve as a runnable acceptance gate, and document design decisions so future scheme resolvers and fan behaviors have a clear regression test.
novelty: An acceptance test written as a standalone script with a global pass counter, extensive inline specification comments, and a live-vs-mocked dual path that lets it prove behavior even when the model service is unavailable.
contribution: A runnable acceptance test that encodes the expected contract for address resolution scheme dispatch and the run_items one-role-over-many-units fan, including per-unit addressing, fail-loud and skip semantics, telemetry shape, and additive preservation of existing primitives.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script for runtime/cognition.py that verifies resolve_address scheme dispatch and run_items one-role-over-N-units fan semantics with live-or-mocked transport.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a runnable regression gate and executable specification for the new resolve_address and run_items cognition primitives while ensuring existing swarm, jury, role, and reduce functions remain unchanged.

symbols.does: A simple assertion helper that increments a global pass counter.
symbols.description: Takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL message, increments the global PASS counter, and prints an ok line. It is the minimal test harness for this script.

symbols.does: Seeds a value at a run:// address using FsStore.
symbols.description: Puts content into the store to obtain a CAS address, then sets a run:// reference to that CAS, returning the CAS address. It mimics how run_swarm and run_items persist outputs.

symbols.does: Probes whether the resident 4B model service is reachable.
symbols.description: Opens an OpenAI-compatible transport pointing at cognition.RESIDENT_BASE_URL and attempts a tiny completion with cognition.RESIDENT_MODEL; any exception means the live path is unavailable.

symbols.does: A tiny mock response class used in the run_items mock branch.
symbols.description: Wraps a dictionary so it can be returned as if it were a validated Pydantic-like model, exposing model_dump for downstream code.
Initializes the mock response instance by copying the supplied dictionary into self._d.

symbols.does: Returns a copy of the wrapped dictionary.
symbols.description: Provides a Pydantic-compatible model_dump method so mocked outputs can be consumed by callers expecting validated models.

symbols.does: Mock completion function that records the last user message content.
symbols.description: Used in the run_items live-fallback branch; appends the final user message content to seen_users and returns a schema-valid stub with intent and an empty which_roles list.

symbols.does: A tiny mock response class used in the fail-loud mock branch.
symbols.description: Wraps a dictionary and exposes model_dump so the mocked fabric client.complete can return a plausible role output.
Initializes the mock response instance by copying the supplied dictionary into self._d.

symbols.does: Returns a copy of the wrapped dictionary.
symbols.description: Provides a Pydantic-compatible model_dump method for the fail-loud mock response.

symbols.does: Mock completion function that returns a fixed stub for the fail-loud test.
symbols.description: Ignores the conversation and always returns a minimal valid focus output so the test can isolate the address-resolution failure path.

symbols.does: A tiny mock response class used in the on_missing skip branch.
symbols.description: Wraps a dictionary and exposes model_dump so the mocked fabric client.complete can return a plausible role output.
Initializes the mock response instance by copying the supplied dictionary into self._d.

symbols.does: Returns a copy of the wrapped dictionary.
symbols.description: Provides a Pydantic-compatible model_dump method for the skip-branch mock response.

symbols.does: Mock completion function that returns a fixed stub for the skip test.
symbols.description: Ignores the conversation and always returns a minimal valid focus output so the test can verify that a missing unit is recorded in skipped while the resolvable unit still maps.
